channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: When is The Wish Upon a Jirachi Event in Pokémon Pokopia? Exact Start Time & End Date
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Pokopia fans are preparing for an influx of new content following the recently announced Expansion Pass, but before that gets underway, the Wish Upon a Jirachi event this week brings new debuts.

After previous events in Pokopia introduced new Pokémon like Hoppip, Skiploom, Jumpluff, and Sableye into the fray, Jirachi is now making its own debut in the title, alongside new cosmetics.

If you’re keen to know exactly when the Jirachi event kicks off in Pokopia, Insider Gaming has all the details.

When Does The Wish Upon a Jirachi Event Begin?

Jirachi arrives in Pokémon Pokopia in the Wish Upon a Jirachi event on Tuesday, June 23, with its debut beginning at 5 AM local time.

Unlike previous events, you can’t time skip to access the Wish Upon a Jirachi event ahead of time, and you can’t roll back the clocks after the event concludes to experience it again.

  • Related: All New Mega Pokémon & Abilities in Pokémon Champions Regulation M-B

Similar to the other events, Jirachi’s arrival comes alongside a new item to collect. Interact with other Pokémon to receive Wish Notes, and complete the requests to transform them into Sparkling Wish Notes, which can then be exchanged with Jirachi inside a reconstructed Pokémon Center for new furniture and decor.

Of course, if you want Jirachi to stick around, you’ll need to build the Pokémon a Habitat, so keep an eye on exactly what the Mythical Pokémon needs.

When Does The Wish Upon a Jirachi Event End?

The Wish Upon a Jirachi event in Pokémon Pokopia will be available until July 8, 2026, at 4:59 AM local time, giving you plenty of time to complete everything there is to offer.

Admittedly, there’s unlikely to be an awful lot to sink your teeth into, if past events are any indication, and once you have a habitat built for Jirachi and unlock all of the available items, there won’t be too much more.

  • Related: All New Pokémon & Held Items in Pokémon Champions in Regulation M-B Update

However, the event is merely a drop in the ocean of what is to come in Pokémon Pokopia following the announcement of post-launch content, split into three parts, the first of which lands in August.

Alongside a free update unlocking underwater exploration, the new Expansion Pass adds a new town to explore and rebuild, alongside a slew of new Pokémon to meet.

Check out the trailer for the Expansion Pass below.

What are you most excited to see added in Pokémon Pokopia? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, find out how to get 100 free Quick Coupons in Pokémon Champions, and don’t forget to sign up for the Insider Gaming newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/pokemon-pokopia-wish-upon-a-jirachi/

item.title: New Marvel ARPG ‘Project Comet’ Leaks Surface Online
item.content:encoded:
A newly announced Marvel title has begun leaking online, revealing playable characters, voice lines, character models, and fresh gameplay details.

Known as Project Comet, the game is reportedly being developed by Scopely, the studio behind Marvel Strike Force, Monopoly GO, and Star Trek Fleet Command. The title appears to be another mobile-focused Marvel experience, this time built around replayable co-op missions and gacha mechanics.

More Project Comet Details

Outside of an initial reveal by industry insider Miller Ross on X earlier this month, very little was known about Project Comet. A closed playtest for iOS and Android users was scheduled for June, but details remained scarce.

That changed over the past few days, as leaker MultiverSusie on X shared a large collection of assets and information from the game, including character models, voice lines, and gameplay descriptions.

  • Related: How To Earn 2500 Free Units In Marvel Rivals—Summer Festival 2026

According to the leaks, Project Comet will feature gacha-style mechanics similar to Genshin Impact, allowing players to unlock and build teams of Marvel heroes and villains. The game is also said to draw inspiration from the Marvel Ultimate Alliance series, with replayable four-player co-op missions and higher difficulty options, presumably offering greater rewards.

The leaked roster includes fan-favourites such as Captain America, Spider-Man, and Wolverine, alongside more niche characters like Elektra, Juggernaut, and Silver Samurai. Check out some of them below, including a snippet of gameplay:

Judging by the leaked UI, each character will have a host of usable abilities, and you’ll be able to activate them in the usual mobile-game manner by pressing them on-screen. It’s unclear whether the game will arrive on PC, with no mention of the platform as things stand.

Interestingly, several of the leaked character models bear a strong resemblance to their Marvel Rivals counterparts; that’s reportedly because some artists who worked on Marvel Rivals are also contributing to Project Comet.

There’s an incredible amount of leaks to pore through, so if you want a closer look, consider scrolling through MultiverSusie’s X account and investigating them for yourself. In the meantime, check out the character reveal trailer for Cyclops in Marvel Rivals—will he be playable in Project Comet?

What do you think of the leaks? Does the game sound promising, or does the mobile premise put you off? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about the expected price for GTA 6, and don’t forget to sign up for our Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: What Time Does Fortnite OG Season 9 Release? Exact Start Time & Date
item.content:encoded:
After two weeks of enjoying the content added in Fortnite Chapter 7, Season 3, even more fun is being added by Epic Games with the start of Fortnite OG Season 9.

The latest seasonal shift is rolling back the clock to another iconic original Fortnite season, giving fans who want a nostalgic experience in the Epic Games title exactly that.

If you’re keen to know exactly when Fortnite OG Season 9 kicks off, Insider Gaming has all the details.

When Time Does Fortnite OG Season 9 Release?

Fortnite OG Season 9 is set to begin on Thursday, June 25, but the exact time the action kicks off is, as usual, not being revealed by Epic Games.

Instead, downtime will begin at the usual time of around 1 AM PT on Thursday morning, which is expected to last for around three to four hours.

  • Related: Poncle Rethinks Fortnite Crossover After Epic’s AI Push

Mid-season updates don’t usually result in the same lengthy downtime as bigger updates, like the change of an entire season, but, as the first update since the start of Chapter 7, Season 3, there could be a few issues to iron out.

In the table below, we’ve outlined the expected downtime start in major regions across the world.

What to Expect in Fortnite OG Season 9

The major changes coming in the next Fortnite update are, of course, the seasonal shift in Fortnite OG, bringing around the start of Season 9.

The new season is based around the original Season 9 in Fortnite, which saw Polar Peak melting and unveiling a giant Kaiju, The Devourer, and subsequently constructing Mecha Team Leader to defeat it in a major live event, which will almost certainly be revisited at the end of the season.

  • Related: How to Get Free Vinicius Jr. Fortnite Baila Emote

Outside of Fortnite OG, the main Battle Royale mode is receiving a fresh influx of Sprites to add to your collection, including a football-themed Sprite to coincide with the World Cup.

As ever, the new update will also bring about the introduction of new cosmetics into the Item Shop, including the recently revealed Krypto sidekick.

What are you most excited to see in Fortnite OG Season 9? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at how players are spending real money on Sprites, and don’t forget to sign up for the Insider Gaming newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For June 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, June 22, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Medication
  • Hint 2: Comes in the form of a tablet
  • Hint 3: Usually used to help with sore throats or a cough

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Medication
  • Dose
  • Mint
  • Cough
  • Drop
  • Medicine
  • Capsule
  • Pill
  • Tablet

Today’s Contexto Answer For June 22, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Lozenge

A Lozenge is a small, medicated tablet that dissolves slowly in the mouth.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on June 23.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/todays-contexto-hints-answer-for-june-22-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: CD Projekt Red Thinks Cyberpunk 2077 Failure Hasn’t Fully Recovered
item.content:encoded:
In 2020, CD Projekt Red, the creator of The Witcher line of games, released Cyberpunk 2077. It quickly became branded as one of the worst games ever made, purely because it was such a buggy mess, fraught with glitches, optimization issues, and game-breaking pitfalls. It was so bad it faced investor lawsuits.

Fast-forward to now, and Cyberpunk 2077 is regarded as a must-play game that everyone should experience, but it took hundreds of millions of dollars to recover it. Unfortunately, the head honchos at CD Projekt Red still feel that, almost six years later, the pain of Cyberpunk’s failure hasn’t left the minds of gamers.

Has Cyberpunk 2077 Redeemed Itself? CDPR Isn’t Sure

In a recent interview with EDGE for the Knowledge newsletter, co-CEO of CD Projekt Red, Michal Nowakowski, sat down to talk about the company’s ‘redemption arc’ beyond Cyberpunk 2077. It’s no big secret that the studio worked extremely hard to revitalize Cyberpunk 2077, but it was an incredibly tough journey for all involved.

  • RELATED: Cyberpunk Edgerunners 2 First Look Coming Soon

Nowakowski reflected on the ‘heartbreak’ that surrounded the game’s launch, which forever marred the studio’s image and left fans of the company with a sour taste in their mouths. It even has the potential to ever so slightly derail the impending release of The Witcher 4, with fans anticipating something of a deja-vu moment.

In the interview, Nowakowski admitted:

I’m not 100 per cent convinced we went through the full redemption arc.

I’m convinced that we lost the faith of some people indefinitely, and that’s a fair thing. But I do hope we will be able to make it back – if not with The Witcher 4, then with whatever comes next.

He took a deep dive into the ten-year roadmap the studio has, packed with a slate of top-tier titles that will hopefully take better shape than the beleagured 2020 open-world shooter. Nowakowski addressed the state of the industry right now, even with an optimistic outlook and a fine repository of games being lined up:

There’s an unprecedented number of games being launched every year, and the fight for attention is tougher than it ever was. In the end, whether you can continue making games largely depends on whether you were successful enough to fund another project of your own. If you’re lean and cheap, and are able to really target the group you want and live off that, then you’re in a good spot.

For many, Cyberpunk 2077 absolutely became one of the most important games ever made. If not for how it eventually shaped up, but for how it was a testament to the dedication and resilience of a studio hellbent on not letting a project fail. It might have cost hundreds of millions of dollars to bring it back to life, but the consensus is that it was money well spent.

Have you ever played Cyberpunk 2077? Let us know what you thought about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Fallout: Forgotten Valley is a dark chapter in the series, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Fallout: Forgotten Valley Could Be The Darkest Way to Experience the Wasteland
item.content:encoded:
If you’re hungry for more Fallout experiences, then I’ve got a doozy for you. It’s called Fallout: Forgotten Valley, and it’s a sizeable mod with some interesting history. It was first released way back in 2011 by a solo developer, and it has been brought back to life this month with fresh localization for an entirely new audience.

In Fallout: Forgotten Valley, New Vegas players will travel to a dark and oppressive new location of the same name, exploring what could be one of the most morbid areas in a Fallout title to date.

Fallout’s Modders Make Magic

Fallout’s modding scene is alive and well in 2026, and that’s clear from some of the hugely ambitious efforts we’re seeing surfacing right now. Forgotten Valley is another entry to the lineup, and it builds on fifteen years of mod history.

  • RELATED: Fallout 3 Remastered Mod Appears Online

The mod’s creator, TheMalkavian, explained that the mod was first uploaded in 2011 in Russian, but now, it’s available in English, opening up a door for an entirely new audience. It’s a fresh upload that has been tested more thoroughly, but the creator offers a fair warning: it’s not very well optimized.

On Nexus Mods, the description reads:

The main storyline is primarily focused on exploration and discovery rather than deep narrative branching, complex dialogue systems, or nonlinear quest design. However, within the Valley you will encounter a small number of friendly NPCs, merchants, and several quest-related characters.

In his breakdown, TheMalkavian reveals the inspiration behind Forgotten Valley. It’s heavily motivated by franchises like STALKER, Twin Peaks, and Evil Dead, and it ‘represents the personal vision of a 90s kid trying to build his own game world.’

It might not be perfect, but you need to give some huge props to TheMalkavian for sticking at it over all these years. The Fallout series is in a boom right now amid the success of the television series, and it’s the perfect time to explore ambitious mods like this one. The screenshots and videos available on Nexus will help you understand how dark and chilling it really is.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if Forgotten Valley looks like your kind of Fallout mod.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Meccha Chameleon has hit a new player record, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Meccha Chameleon Hits New Player Peak After Hitting Seven Million Sales
item.content:encoded:
Meccha Chameleon is the new talking point of the day in the gaming space. The hilarious and inventive ‘prop hunt’ style game launched just under two weeks ago to a hugely popular reception, launching up the charts and securing a whopping seven million sales in just twelve days.

On Steam, the game has catapulted its way up the ranks into the top spots, securing a new peak player count just hours ago, at the time of writing. The game managed to pull in a whopping 340,534 concurrent players, drawing it level with the likes of Resident Evil Requiem, Starfield, Destiny 2, and GTA 5.

Meccha Chameleon is a Sensation

Meccha Chameleon is successful for one solid reason, and that’s the backing of streamers. It’s no secret that games these days face a make-or-break moment when they hit the market, and the investment of the content creation crew usually determines that decision. If the big streamers latch onto your game, you’re successful, at least in the short term.

That’s what happened with Meccha Chameleon, a prop hunt-style game with a twist. Instead of simply hiding as various items in the environment, players must use paint to disfigure and manipulate their character, blending them into the background or masquerading as anything they find on the map.

It’s a low-cost, low-frills game with huge potential, and even though it might seem gimmicky, it’s doing remarkably well right now.

  • RELATED: How Much Does Meccha Chameleon Cost?

Remarkably, the game was assembled by a solo Japanese developer, who has been dealing with the weight of a smash hit success by releasing updates and bug fixes almost daily. Hours ago, that developer revealed a new sales milestone:

Have you participated in the overnight sensation that is Meccha Chameleon? Let us know your thoughts about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that a Wolverine actor is now in GTA 6, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: ‘Wolverine’ Actor Lands Role in Grand Theft Auto 6
item.content:encoded:
The notion of who’s who in Grand Theft Auto 6 is one of the most talked-about topics in recent years. Every time Rockstar Games and Take-Two Interactive unveil some new media, everyone starts digging into who could possibly be playing the characters slowly being revealed in the game that’s coming later this year.

In the most recent discovery, eagle-eyed fans learned that Brett Gipson, the actor behind Sabertooth in the upcoming Wolverine, released in September, has a supporting role in Grand Theft Auto 6.

Brett Gipson is in GTA 6 as ‘Ellis’

Brett Gipson, who’ll be bursting onto PlayStation consoles in September as Sabertooth in the highly anticipated release of Marvel’s Wolverine, has a role in Grand Theft Auto 6. This is shaping up to be one hell of a year for him where a payday is concerned, as he was also in Netflix’s new Stranger Things game, and he had a role in Borderlands 4.

In Gipson’s resumé, available on Actors Access, it’s stated clearly that he voices a character called Ellis in Grand Theft Auto 6, which is slated to drop on November 19th.

This isn’t a character that has been revealed, so fans naturally took to social media to start piecing together their predictions. The scope of Grand Theft Auto 6 is so staggering that we’ll likely see dozens, if not hundreds, of these revelations surface in the coming months.

  • RELATED: GTA 6 Release Date Disappeared and Folks Panicked

If there’s one thing Rockstar Games loves to do, it’s weaving cameos into Grand Theft Auto games. Brett Gipson is likely the first in a long line of established actors yet to reveal roles in GTA 6.

Gipson only recently got into video games, and much of his career has been spent in traditional film and television. His resumé is already sparkling in the gaming space, though. His debut slate has taken him across some of the biggest franchises in the modern entertainment space, and that bodes very well.

(Thanks to MP1st for the resumé spot)

Are you eager to see Brett Gipson’s performance in GTA 6? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Ubisoft’s co-founder was killed in a plane crash, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1107)—Answers for Monday, June 22
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to add another notch onto that NYT Connections streak. In this guide, you’ll find the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to power through the puzzle and come out the other side without too much fuss. If you’re stuck on today’s NYT Connections challenge, simply refer to the hints below!

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections answers you need!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (June 22)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • STRESSED
  • BOOMER
  • POWDER
  • HEAD
  • ALPHA
  • SOFT
  • X
  • LEAD
  • TIMES
  • PRIMARY
  • SILENT
  • ●
  • SHORT
  • POPSICLE
  • BY
  • BANGKOK

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Words used to describe someone in charge
  • Green – Ways to refer to multiplication
  • Blue – Ways you might say something
  • Purple – Words that start with onomatopoeia

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, June 22!

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, June 22 (#1107)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – ALPHA, HEAD, LEAD, PRIMARY
  • Green – BY, TIMES, X, ●
  • Blue – SHORT, SILENT, SOFT, STRESSED
  • Purple – BANGKOK, BOOMER, POPSICLE, POWDER

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: “There was a lot of pain, but today it feels like it was all worth it” – NiKo reflects on IEM Cologne 2026 Major Triumph
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming spoke to Nikola ‘NiKo’ Kovač after the legendary rifler claimed his first Major title at the IEM Cologne 2026 Major.

Falcons defeated Furia 13-8, 13-8, 13-8 in the Grand Final, completing a historic run which saw Falcons defeat G2, Monte, NAVI, Vitality, Spirit, and FURIA to make history in Cologne, finally delivering a Major title to one of Counter-Strike’s greatest ever players.

Insider Gaming interviews NiKo after Falcons win the IEM Cologne 2026 Major

You won the major, you lift the trophy, everyone is chanting your name. I think everyone knows what you’ve been through. How beautiful was that moment?

Definitely the best moment in my career, nothing can describe this feeling, I think everyone could see my emotions when I was walking towards the trophy, at moments I couldn’t believe that it was happening. At moments, I had a bit of flashbacks from 2023.

I went through a lot in my career; people that follow me know how hard it was, how much I’ve suffered. There was a lot of pain; I lost a lot in my career, but today it feels like it was all worth it, all of that was a lesson, and all of that was leading up to something great.

I just have a huge relief right now, and I hope that this is just the beginning.

Not only did you win the Major on Inferno, the site of pain in 2018, but you did so with a smile on your face. It felt like a different NiKo on stage. There was so much confidence. How did you feel?

I felt great, I felt great in the whole playoffs, I felt great going into each game, I had a huge trust in the team, and the team believed in me as well.

I felt like the team gave me the space to play the game that I wanted to play, I was aggressive and I needed to be aggressive, close out the rounds when I needed to close out the rounds,.

It’s a great feeling that I can be able to display such a game in the biggest event in my life. I’m really happy with how I approached things throughout the whole major.

All of these things in the past, I think today, maybe not today, but throughout the playoffs, I kind of maybe cracked a code of what I need to do in order to achieve even greater things.

We were talking before the playoffs about you in those lurking positions, and I felt like you had so much impact in those roles today. Did you really have a feel for all of those things in this final?

Yeah, definitely, I did. When I feel confident, then it’s easier to make those plays as well, but it’s also thanks to Finn [karrigan] and the system that he has put me in.

I feel like I have more space to do those lurks if that makes sense. I just want to get better now with this role because this is the role that I have right now, and I think I can do even better than what I did at this event, with more confidence and more consistency.

I’m just happy that I could display such CS.

This is only 60 or so days into this roster. Do you still feel like you have a long way to go to develop within the team? How far can this team go?

I really don’t want to think too much ahead. I want to enjoy this moment because it’s been my dream, and I just need to collect my thoughts tomorrow and just enjoy it to the fullest.

I believe that this line-up, I believed in the very beginning when I joined Falcons that we can create something great, and today was a huge step towards it.

Now it’s a player break, so it might be hard to carry the confidence and the momentum towards the next event. But I have a huge belief in this team now, we have full trust in each other right now, and I just feel like everyone is feeling very comfortable in their roles, which is the biggest, biggest or the most important thing for the team.

If you look at Vitality, everyone is playing at a very high level, anyone can carry the game, everyone is doing his best, and I think with this line-up we can do the same.

Looking forward to a break and then back to work.

  • Related: Opinion: No, the Vitality Era is Not “Over”

End of Interview

Let us know your IEM Cologne 2026 Major predictions on Insider Gaming’s Discord


For other news, see the League of Legends MSI play-ins bracket and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.
